---
title: 'Dear Her 2/25/2020'
date: '2020-02-25T15:21:00.0010000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: '(My Journal) Fed By Faith'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '15299340786757796'
blogger_filename: '/2020/02/dear-her-2252020.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/(My Journal) Fed By Faith/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Dear Her'
---

Okay.... So this will sound odd if it doesn't happen. But... Maybe I am just being stretched in my willingness. Who knows.&nbsp;<div><br></div><div>Ever since I started reading Where is God when it Hurts, by Philip Yancey, I have had a recurring thought that won't go away.</div><div><br></div><div>What if God is calling me to love someone who has a "disability"? Like someone with a spinal cord injury, wheelchair bound.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>I saw this awesome video of a guy who is a Maker, and he got engaged to a paraplegic woman. He made a wedding ring out of one of her old wheel chairs. He made her an awesome new chair. He put an elevator into the house for her. They're so inspiring.</div><div><br></div><div>The story of Joni Eareckson Tada is captivating. She was a total quadriplegic and she still got married afterwards. That one is hard for me, but if God asks me to drink from that cup (before or after we marry) I will drink it.</div><div><br></div><div>Maybe none of this is true. Maybe you aren't and never will be injured in this way. Maybe it's just an exercise to stretch my capacity to love.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>But I think I can honestly say yes, I would date and marry a paraplegic if that's who he had for me.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>I spent time researching if they can still have sex, ha ha... Apparently 53% of women with Spinal Cord Injury can still orgasm. Good to know.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>This is probably the weirdest letter ive written to you. Maybe it'll never become relevant. But I'm preparing to love you, whatever that may mean.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>Your Lover, Darrell</div><div><br></div><div><br></div><div><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;">
  <a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/1582644105305760-0-a8a1eba2ada9.png" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;">
    <img border="0"   src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/1582644105305760-0-a8a1eba2ada9.png" width="400">
  </a>
</div><br></div>
